Name: CardFilter
Summary: A function used to filter cards.
Description: A function used to filter cards.

DeclaredType.name: CardFilter
DeclaredType.summary: A function used to filter cards.
DeclaredType.description: A function used to filter cards.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The card to check.
Additional arguments, usually passed by the function that's using the filter.

DeclaredType.guide:
A `CardFilter` is a function that takes a `Card` as its first parameter and returns a boolean (either `true` or `false`). A simple example would be:

```lua
function s.lvfilter(c)
  return c:IsMonster() and c:IsLevel(4)
end
```

This returns `true` if `c` is a Level 4 monster.

## Using filters
Filters are mostly used with certain `Duel` and `Group` functions that collect or check for cards that fulfill the filter (in other words, cards that make the function return `true`). Those functions will call the filter passed to them for every card they are concerned with.

For example, we can use the `s.lvfilter` we have earlier to check if there are cards that fulfill it in certain locations. The following expression will be `true` if there is at least 1 Level 4 monster in either player's GY:
```lua
Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard(s.lvfilter,tp,LOCATION_GRAVE,LOCATION_GRAVE,1,nil)
```

We can also make a player select cards that fulfill a filter. Here, a player will select 1 Level 4 monster in their hand, and the selected card will be stored in a group called `g`:
```lua
local g=Duel.SelectMatchingCard(tp,s.lvfilter,LOCATION_HAND,0,1,1,nil)
```

## Additional `CardFilter` Arguments
Sometimes, filters need additional details other than the card they're checking. For example, we may need to write a filter for a face-up monster with ATK higher than the activating player's LP - a value that changes throughout the duel. This is where having additional filter arguments comes in.

Filter functions always take a card as their first parameter (usually named `c` by convention), but they can take any amount of arguments after that. For the earlier example, we can create a filter that has an additional parameter called `minatk`. We then check inside the filter if `c`'s ATK is greater than or equal to `minatk`.

```lua
function s.atkfilter(c,minatk)
  return c:IsFaceup() and c:IsAttackAbove(minatk)
end
```

Now, for the filter to know what `minatk` is, **it needs to be passed to it**. This part is important. Otherwise, `minatk` would just be `nil`. Functions that use filters can take additional arguments. These functions will pass the additional arguments they receive (usually after the "exception" argument) to the filter they're using, and they become additional arguments of that filter.

To illustrate, say we have an effect with an activation condition that says *If you control a face-up monster with ATK higher than your LP:*. For this, we can use the `s.atkfilter` earlier like so:

```lua
function s.condition(e,tp,eg,ep,ev,re,r,rp)
  local lp=Duel.GetLP(tp)
  return Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard(s.atkfilter,tp,LOCATION_MZONE,0,1,nil,lp)
end
```

Here, we first obtained `tp`'s LP and assigned it to the variable named `lp`. Then, we passed that `lp` as an extra argument to `Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard`. Note that it counts as an extra argument since it comes after the "exception" argument, which is `nil` in this case.

Internally, `Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard` will call `s.atkfilter` using `lp` as an extra argument. This will correspond to the `minatk` parameter of the filter. In other words, the first extra argument passed to `Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard` (called `lp`)
becomes the second argument passed to `s.atkfilter` (called `minatk`).

## Nesting `CardFilter` functions
There are also cases where a filter needs additional information that depends on another `Card` that fulfills a separate filter. A simple example is an effect that needs to banish 1 monster from a player's hand, and send another monster from that player's Deck to the GY with the same level as the first monster.

This means that the second monster's level will depend on the first monster's level. Naturally, we would need two filters for this, but more importantly, the first monster's filter will need to call `Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard` inside it using the second monster's filter.

```lua
--filter for the first monster (the card to be banished)
function s.rmfilter(c,tp)
  return c:IsMonster() and c:HasLevel() and c:IsAbleToRemove()
    and Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard(s.tgfilter,tp,LOCATION_DECK,0,1,nil,c:GetLevel())
end
--filter for the second monster (the card to be sent to the GY)
function s.tgfilter(c,lv)
  return c:IsMonster() and c:IsAbleToGrave() and c:IsLevel(lv)
end
```

Here, `s.rmfilter` is fulfilled if `c` is a monster that has a level and can be banished, and that there exists a monster in `tp`'s Deck which satisfies `s.tgfilter`. It passes `c:GetLevel()` (the monster's level) as an additional argument to the `Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard` call, which will in turn pass it to `s.tgfilter` as its additional argument called `lv`.

`s.tgfilter` will then be fulfilled by monsters that can be sent to the GY and has a level equal to the passed `lv`. The following legality check can be used to see if we have a valid monster to banish (such that we'll also have a valid monster to send to the GY):

```lua
if chk==0 then return Duel.IsExistingMatchingCard(s.rmfilter,tp,LOCATION_HAND,0,1,nil,tp) end
```

Essentially, we're using a function that uses a filter that uses a function that uses a filter. Hence, "nested". In theory, there's no limit to how deep such nesting could go (ignoring technical limitations like memory, performance, and stack size), but in practice, most cards that require nesting filters will only need single nesting. For a card that uses nesting filters two levels deep, you can check out [<u>Small World</u>](https://github.com/ProjectIgnis/CardScripts/blob/master/official/c89558743.lua).

returns.description: `true` if the card fulfills the filter, `false` otherwise.

Name: DelayedEffectCondition
Summary: A condition to check affected cards in a given phase.
Description: A condition to check affected cards in a given phase.

DeclaredType.name: DelayedEffectCondition
DeclaredType.summary: A condition to check affected cards in a given phase.
DeclaredType.description: A condition to check affected cards in a given phase.

parameters.name: ag
parameters.description: The group of affected cards.

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The "delayed effect" that needs to check the cards.

parameters.name: tp
parameters.description: Unused, always `0`.

parameters.name: eg
parameters.description: Unused.

parameters.name: ep
parameters.description: The current player.

parameters.name: ev
parameters.description: Unused, always `0`.

parameters.name: re
parameters.description: Unused.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: Unused, always `0`.

parameters.name: rp
parameters.description: Unused, always `2`.

DeclaredType.guide:
::: warning
Since the delayed effect is an `EFFECT_TYPE_FIELD+EFFECT_TYPE_CONTINUOUS` effect,
`tp`, `eg`, `ev`, `re`, `r`, `rp` are practically useless.
They're either always `nil`/`0` or redundant information.

This function type's signature may be adjusted later.
:::

Name: Effect
Summary: Represents an effect.
Description: Represents an effect. Can use the functions in the [`Effect` namespace](/api/namespaces/Effect) as methods. This type is not limited to what are officially referred to as ["effects"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Effect) in *Yu-Gi-Oh!*. It is also used to implement ["conditions"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Condition) and even some Duel rules.

DeclaredType.name: Effect
DeclaredType.summary: Represents an effect.
DeclaredType.description: Represents an effect. Can use the functions in the [`Effect` namespace](/api/namespaces/Effect) as methods. This type is not limited to what are officially referred to as ["effects"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Effect) in *Yu-Gi-Oh!*. It is also used to implement ["conditions"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Condition) and even some Duel rules.

Name: EffectArray
Summary: An array containing effects.
Description: An [array-like table](https://www.lua.org/pil/11.1.html) containing [Effect](/api/types/Effect) values.

DeclaredType.name: EffectArray
DeclaredType.summary: An array containing effects.
DeclaredType.description: An [array-like table](https://www.lua.org/pil/11.1.html) containing [Effect](/api/types/Effect) values.

Name: EffectCondition
Summary: Checks an activated effect's condition.
Description: A function used to check an activated effect's condition, usually passed to [`Effect.SetCondition`](/api/functions/Effect/SetCondition). Conditions refer to those explicitly stated in the card text, (e.g. "If you control a monster:"), not for activation legality (e.g. checking if you have a Field Spell in your Deck to activate Terraforming), which is checked by the [EffectTarget](/api/types/EffectTarget) instead.

DeclaredType.name: EffectCondition
DeclaredType.summary: Checks an activated effect's condition.
DeclaredType.description: A function used to check an activated effect's condition, usually passed to [`Effect.SetCondition`](/api/functions/Effect/SetCondition). Conditions refer to those explicitly stated in the card text, (e.g. "If you control a monster:"), not for activation legality (e.g. checking if you have a Field Spell in your Deck to activate Terraforming), which is checked by the [EffectTarget](/api/types/EffectTarget) instead.

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The effect being checked.

parameters.name: tp
parameters.description: The player activating the effect.

parameters.name: eg
parameters.description: A group of cards associated to the event that prompted the effect activation. The specifics of this group vary depending on the [event code](/api/enums/Event).

parameters.name: ep
parameters.description: The player associated to the event that prompted the effect activation.

parameters.name: ev
parameters.description: A value associated to the event that prompted the effect activation. The specifics of this value vary depending on the event code.

parameters.name: re
parameters.description: The effect that caused the event that prompted the effect activation.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: Composite [Reason](/api/enums/Reason) value of the event that prompted the effect activation.

parameters.name: rp
parameters.description: The player that caused the event that prompted the effect activation.

returns.description: If the effect can be activated.

parameters.name: rp

parameters.name: chk
parameters.description: Must be `0` when checking if the cost can be paid, and `1` when performing the cost.

returns.description: If `chk` is `0`, returns `true` if the cost can be paid.

Description: A function used to filter what can be used by a [Rank-Up-Magic](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Rank-Up-Magic) to Special Summon an Xyz Monster.

DeclaredType.name: RankUpMaterialFilter
DeclaredType.summary: Filters materials to be used for a Special Summon by Rank-Up-Magic
DeclaredType.description: A function used to filter what can be used by a [Rank-Up-Magic](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Rank-Up-Magic) to Special Summon an Xyz Monster.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The material to check.

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The effect performing the Special Summon.

returns.description: If the material passed the filter.

Name: RankUpSummonRegister
Summary: Registers an Rank-Up-Magic Special Summon procedure
Description: A function that registers a Special Summon procedure using a [Rank-Up-Magic](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Rank-Up-Magic) effect.

DeclaredType.name: RankUpSummonRegister
DeclaredType.summary: Registers an Rank-Up-Magic Special Summon procedure
DeclaredType.description: A function that registers a Special Summon procedure using a [Rank-Up-Magic](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Rank-Up-Magic) effect.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The card to register the procedure to.

returns.description: The registered effect.

Name: RitualMaterialFilter
Summary: Filters Ritual materials in Ritual Summons
Description: A function used to filter Ritual materials in Ritual Summons

DeclaredType.name: RitualMaterialFilter
DeclaredType.summary: Filters Ritual materials in Ritual Summons
DeclaredType.description: A function used to filter Ritual materials in Ritual Summons

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The material to check.

parameters.name: tp
parameters.description: The player performing the Ritual Summon.

returns.description: If the material passed the filter.

Name: Script
Summary: The card script metatable
Description: The card script metatable, where values shared by all copies of a card are defined.

DeclaredType.name: Script
DeclaredType.summary: The card script metatable
DeclaredType.description: The card script metatable, where values shared by all copies of a card are defined.

Name: string
Summary: The lua string type.
Description: The lua [string](https://www.lua.org/pil/2.4.html) type.

DeclaredType.name: string
DeclaredType.summary: The lua string type.
DeclaredType.description: The lua [string](https://www.lua.org/pil/2.4.html) type.

Name: SubgroupCheck
Summary: A function used to check subgroups of a group.
Description: A function used to check subgroups of a group.

DeclaredType.name: SubgroupCheck
DeclaredType.summary: A function used to check subgroups of a group.
DeclaredType.description: A function used to check subgroups of a group.

parameters.name: sg
parameters.description: The subgroup being checked.

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The effect performing the check.

parameters.name: tp
parameters.description: The player performing the check.

parameters.name: mg
parameters.description: The full group that `sg` belongs to.

returns.description: If the subgroup fulfills the check.
If true, asserts that no larger subgroup in `mg` will ever fulfill the check.

Name: table
Summary: The lua table type.
Description: The lua [table](https://www.lua.org/pil/2.5.html) type.

DeclaredType.name: table
DeclaredType.summary: The lua table type.
DeclaredType.description: The lua [table](https://www.lua.org/pil/2.5.html) type.

Name: TargetFilterFunction
Summary: Filters cards affected by an effect
Description: A function used to filter cards affected by an effect. Usually passed to [`Effect.SetTarget`](/api/functions/Effect/SetTarget) for non-activated effects that affect certain cards.

DeclaredType.name: TargetFilterFunction
DeclaredType.summary: Filters cards affected by an effect
DeclaredType.description: A function used to filter cards affected by an effect. Usually passed to [`Effect.SetTarget`](/api/functions/Effect/SetTarget) for non-activated effects that affect certain cards.

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The effect checking the card.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The card to check.

returns.description: If the card passed the filter.

Name: userdata
Summary: The lua userdata type.

DeclaredType.name: userdata
DeclaredType.summary: The lua userdata type.
DeclaredType.description: The lua [userdata](https://www.lua.org/pil/2.7.html) type.

Name: XyzExtraCheck
Summary: Additional check to perform on groups of Xyz materials
Description: An additional check to perform on groups of materials to be used for an Xyz Summon (e.g. [Number F0: Utopic Future](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/11413)).

DeclaredType.name: XyzExtraCheck
DeclaredType.summary: Additional check to perform on groups of Xyz materials
DeclaredType.description: An additional check to perform on groups of materials to be used for an Xyz Summon (e.g. [Number F0: Utopic Future](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/11413)).

parameters.name: g
parameters.description: The group of materials to be checked.

parameters.name: tp
parameters.description: The player performing the Xyz Summon.

parameters.name: xyz
parameters.description: The Xyz Monster to summon.

returns.description: If `g` fulfills the check.

Name: XyzExtraOperation
Summary: Additional function to execute when Xyz Summoning.
Description: An additional function to be executed when performing an Xyz Summon, *before* using the Xyz Materials (e.g. [Digital Bug Corebage](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/12327), [Number 99: Utopic Dragon](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/11305))

DeclaredType.name: XyzExtraOperation
DeclaredType.summary: Additional function to execute when Xyz Summoning.
DeclaredType.description: An additional function to be executed when performing an Xyz Summon, *before* using the Xyz Materials (e.g. [Digital Bug Corebage](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/12327), [Number 99: Utopic Dragon](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/11305))

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The Xyz Summon procedure effect.

parameters.name: tp
parameters.description: The player performing the Xyz Summon.

parameters.name: chk
parameters.description: Must be `0` when checking if the operation can be performed, and `1` when executing the operation.

parameters.name: mc
parameters.description: The card being used as material.

returns.description: If `chk` is 0, returns `true` if the operation can be performed.
